sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_dates (id INTEGER, event_date DATE, notes VARCHAR);
INSERT INTO test_dates VALUES (1, DATE '2023-03-01', 'Start of March'), (2, DATE '2023-02-29', 'Leap Year Day'), (3, NULL, 'No Date');
CREATE TABLE date_diff (start_date TIMESTAMP, end_date TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO date_diff VALUES (TIMESTAMP '1992-03-27', TIMESTAMP '1992-03-22'), (TIMESTAMP '2023-03-10', NULL);
SELECT id, dayofweek(event_date) AS weekday FROM test_dates WHERE event_date IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, dayofweek(event_date) AS weekday FROM test_dates WHERE dayofweek(event_date) < 3;
SELECT DISTINCT d.id, dayofweek(d.event_date) - (SELECT MAX(dayofweek(event_date)) FROM test_dates) AS diff FROM test_dates d;
SELECT d1.id, d1.event_date, (SELECT dayofweek(d2.event_date) FROM test_dates d2 WHERE d2.id = d1.id) - dayofweek(d1.event_date) AS day_diff FROM test_dates d1;
SELECT d.id, dayofweek(d.event_date) AS weekday, COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY dayofweek(d.event_date)) AS count_per_day FROM test_dates d GROUP BY d.id, d.event_date;
SELECT d1.id, d1.event_date, (TIMESTAMP '1992-03-27' - TIMESTAMP '1992-03-22') AS interval_diff, dayofweek(d2.event_date) FROM test_dates d1 JOIN date_diff d2 ON d1.id = d2.start_date IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, event_date, (dayofweek(event_date) + 1) % 7 AS adjusted_weekday FROM test_dates HAVING adjusted_weekday = 0;

SELECT id, event_date, (dayofweek(event_date) - (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_dates WHERE event_date IS NOT NULL)) AS weekday_adjusted FROM test_dates WHERE event_date IS NOT NULL ORDER BY weekday_adjusted DESC;
